title: Patta Fort
tagline: Patta Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Patta Fort, or Patta Killa, also known as Vishramgad, is a fort situated between Nasik and Ahmadnagar in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Patta Fort, or Patta Killa, also known as Vishramgad, is a fort situated between Nasik and Ahmadnagar in Maharashtra, India. The Indian ruler Chatrapati Shivaji I Maharaj once visited and rested there. The residents of Patta Killa are known as Pattekar, meaning "residents of Fort Patta". Patta Fort stands approximately 1,392 metres (4,567 ft) above sea level.

topPlaces.name: Kadwa Dam
topPlaces.description: Kadwa Dam, is an earthfill dam on Kadwa river near Igatpuri, Nashik district in state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Bharvir Khurd
topPlaces.description: Bharvir Khurd is a village of Igatpuri Taluka in Nashik District in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is located near the Kadwa Reservoir.

items.note: Kadwa Dam, is an earthfill dam on Kadwa river near Igatpuri, Nashik district in state of Maharashtra in India.
Bharvir Khurd is a village of Igatpuri Taluka in Nashik District in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is located near the Kadwa Reservoir.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Patta Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Patta Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Patta Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (57 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (27 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Patta Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Patta Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Patta Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Patta Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Patta Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kadwa Dam, Bharvir Khurd.
